workOrder.title: Render current site access assignments before management forms

selectionReason: The scoped site-access route already returned active assignment rows, but the workspace only showed save/remove forms. Rendering the current assignments first improves site-admin workflow without route, permission, or mutation changes.
userFacingImprovement: Site admins can confirm active mentor, viewer, program teacher, administration, and site-admin access before changing assignments.
privacyAndPermissionNotes: No API route, query parameter, role grant, permission helper, mutation behavior, tenant boundary, site boundary, program scope, mentor assignment scope, or student visibility changed.
The summary uses only rows already returned by the scoped /api/site/access-assignments response.
Viewer rows are labeled read-only; mentor rows remain assigned-student only; site-admin assignment controls remain hidden when permissions.canAssignSiteAdmins is false.

candidateSummary.decision: deferred; hosted audit writes not allowed
deferred; audit destination shape needs review

validation.notes: Full validation passed; git diff --check reported only CRLF normalization warnings.

doNotRepeat: Do not re-add the Users & Access current assignment summary unless data-site-access-assignment-summary="true" or focused workspace coverage regresses.

ladderHandoff.evidence: Users & Access now renders current scoped assignment rows before save/remove forms from the existing /api/site/access-assignments response.
ladderHandoff.unlocks: Site admins can verify current access before changing it, and future access-history work has a visible read-only summary to build from.
ladderHandoff.next: Run hosted section-level permission proof when allowed; otherwise review access-assignment audit history shape before exposing any timeline.
ladderHandoff.blockers: Hosted proof needs safe fake-account/audit runtime; access-history UI needs audit visibility review; missing-submission queue rows do not exist; archive retry/regenerate needs mutation policy.
